is a department of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute"), and one of the largest [film archives](/wiki/Film_archives "Film archives") in the world. The National Archives collects, preserves, restores, and shares the [films](/wiki/Film "Film") and television programmes which have helped to shape and record British life and times since the development of cine film in the late 19th century. The majority of the collection is British originated material, but it also features internationally significant holdings from around the world. The National Archive also collects films which feature key British actors and the work of British directors.
["Inside the BFI's new film storage facility"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/av/entertainment-arts-14665763). BBC News. 29 August 2011. Retrieved 15 March 2023.

Fabrizi, Elisabetta, 'Is This Cinema?', in Balsom, E., Perks, S., Reynolds, L., (Ed.), 'Artists' Moving Image in Britain from 1989', Paul Mellon Foundation/Yale University Press, 2019.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British Film Institute department

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["BFI National Archive"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22BFI+National+Archive%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22BFI+National+Archive%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22BFI+National+Archive%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22BFI+National+Archive%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22BFI+National+Archive%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22BFI+National+Archive%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2019)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

BFI National Archive

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Map | |
| Former name | * National Film Library (1935–1955) * National Film Archive (1955–1992) * National Film and Television Archive (1992–2006) |
| Established | 1935 (1935) |
| Location | Kingshill Way, [Berkhamsted](/wiki/Berkhamsted "Berkhamsted"), HP4 3TP |
| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [51°45′06″N 0°34′40″W﻿ / ﻿51.75167°N 0.57778°W﻿ / 51.75167; -0.57778](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=BFI_National_Archive&params=51_45_06_N_0_34_40_W_type:landmark) |
| Type | Film and television archive |

The **BFI National Archive** is a department of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute"), and one of the largest [film archives](/wiki/Film_archives "Film archives") in the world. The National Archives collects, preserves, restores, and shares the [films](/wiki/Film "Film") and television programmes which have helped to shape and record British life and times since the development of cine film in the late 19th century. The majority of the collection is British originated material, but it also features internationally significant holdings from around the world. The National Archive also collects films which feature key British actors and the work of British directors.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BFI_National_Archive&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The National Archive was founded in 1935[[1]](#cite_note-KFA-1) as the National Film Library. The archives first curator was [Ernest Lindgren](/wiki/Ernest_Lindgren "Ernest Lindgren"), and the first preservation officer was [Harold Brown](/wiki/Harold_Brown_(film_preservationist) "Harold Brown (film preservationist)").[[1]](#cite_note-KFA-1)

In 1955, the archive became the National Film Archive, and, in 1992, the National Film and Television Archive. It was renamed BFI National Archive in 2006.

## Campuses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BFI_National_Archive&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Campuses")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5a/BFI_New_Master_Film_Store_Gaydon_-_offices.jpg/250px-BFI_New_Master_Film_Store_Gaydon_-_offices.jpg)](/wiki/File:BFI_New_Master_Film_Store_Gaydon_-_offices.jpg)

The Master Film Store of the BFI National Archive, Gaydon, Warwickshire, opened 2011, designed by Cullinan Studio.

The National Archive is based across two sites. The [J. Paul Getty, Jr.](/wiki/Paul_Getty "Paul Getty") Conservation Centre in [Berkhamsted](/wiki/Berkhamsted "Berkhamsted"), [Hertfordshire](/wiki/Hertfordshire "Hertfordshire"), named after its benefactor, is the base for much of the restoration work, while approximately 140 million feet of unstable [nitrate film](/wiki/Nitrocellulose#Film "Nitrocellulose") and all the master film collection held on acetate or other media is kept separately at a BFI storage site at [Gaydon](/wiki/Gaydon "Gaydon") in [Warwickshire](/wiki/Warwickshire "Warwickshire").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Preservation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BFI_National_Archive&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Preservation")]

[Film preservation](/wiki/Film_preservation "Film preservation") is an ongoing project among filmmakers, historians, archivists, museums, and [nonprofit organisations](/wiki/Nonprofit_organization "Nonprofit organization") to rescue deteriorating [film stock](/wiki/Film_stock "Film stock") and preserve the recorded image. In recent years the BFI National Archive has completed a number of much anticipated restorations of a diverse range of film titles. This has included the [Mitchell and Kenyon](/wiki/Mitchell_and_Kenyon "Mitchell and Kenyon") collection, which consists almost entirely of actuality films commissioned by travelling fairground operators for showing at local fairgrounds or other venues across the UK in the early part of the twentieth century.

Other notable recent restorations include:

* [Alfred Hitchcock](/wiki/Alfred_Hitchcock "Alfred Hitchcock")'s nine surviving silent feature films[[3]](#cite_note-3)
* *[Love, Life and Laughter](/wiki/Love,_Life_and_Laughter_(1923_film) "Love, Life and Laughter (1923 film)")* (1923)
* *[The Epic of Everest](/wiki/The_Epic_of_Everest "The Epic of Everest")* (1924)
* *[The Great White Silence](/wiki/The_Great_White_Silence "The Great White Silence")* (1924)
* *[Shooting Stars](/wiki/Shooting_Stars_(1927_film) "Shooting Stars (1927 film)")* (dir. Anthony Asquith, 1927)
* *[The Battles of Coronel and Falkland Islands](/wiki/The_Battles_of_Coronel_and_Falkland_Islands "The Battles of Coronel and Falkland Islands")* (1927)
* *[Shiraz: A Romance of India](/wiki/Shiraz_(film) "Shiraz (film)")* (1928)
* *[The First Born](/wiki/The_First_Born_(1928_film) "The First Born (1928 film)")* (1928)
* *[Underground](/wiki/Underground_(1928_film) "Underground (1928 film)")* (dir. [Anthony Asquith](/wiki/Anthony_Asquith "Anthony Asquith"), 1928)
* *[The Informer](/wiki/The_Informer_(1929_film) "The Informer (1929 film)")* (1929)
* *[The Private Life of Henry VIII](/wiki/The_Private_Life_of_Henry_VIII "The Private Life of Henry VIII")* (1933)
* *[Hell Drivers](/wiki/Hell_Drivers_(film) "Hell Drivers (film)")* (1957)
* *[Night of the Demon](/wiki/Night_of_the_Demon "Night of the Demon")* (1957)
* *[The Day the Earth Caught Fire](/wiki/The_Day_the_Earth_Caught_Fire "The Day the Earth Caught Fire")* (1961)
* *[Women in Love](/wiki/Women_in_Love_(film) "Women in Love (film)")* (1969)
* *[Jabberwocky](/wiki/Jabberwocky_(1977_film) "Jabberwocky (1977 film)")* (1977)

## Collections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BFI_National_Archive&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Collections")]

The BFI National Archive now comprises over 275,000 titles in total consisting of feature, non-fiction, short films (dating from 1894), 210,000 television programmes and some artists' films. It is one of the largest film collections in the world. Notable collections include:

* [Mitchell and Kenyon](/wiki/Mitchell_and_Kenyon "Mitchell and Kenyon") collection
* [GPO Film Unit](/wiki/GPO_Film_Unit "GPO Film Unit") collection
* [Central Office of Information (COI)](/wiki/Central_Office_of_Information "Central Office of Information") film collection
* [Chaplin](/wiki/Charlie_Chaplin "Charlie Chaplin") Out-Takes collection

The archive holds 20,000 silent films including, [Cecil Hepworth](/wiki/Cecil_Hepworth "Cecil Hepworth")'s *[Alice in Wonderland](/wiki/Alice_in_Wonderland_(1903_film) "Alice in Wonderland (1903 film)")* (1903), and actively collects artists' moving images. In addition to moving image materials the Special Collections hold the records of filmmakers and institutions. Significant collections include:

* [Michael Powell](/wiki/Michael_Powell "Michael Powell") and [Emeric Pressburger](/wiki/Emeric_Pressburger "Emeric Pressburger")
* [Carol Reed](/wiki/Carol_Reed "Carol Reed")
* [David Lean](/wiki/David_Lean "David Lean")
* [Ken Loach](/wiki/Ken_Loach "Ken Loach")
* [Derek Jarman](/wiki/Derek_Jarman "Derek Jarman")
* [Alan Parker](/wiki/Alan_Parker "Alan Parker")

Films and television programmes are acquired mainly by donation or, in the case of independent television, via funding direct from the TV companies. Emphasis is placed on British productions but whenever possible important and popular movies from overseas are also acquired. Films from the Archive have also acted as material for the BFI's programme of artists' moving image commissions, most notably through the programme of the [BFI Gallery](/wiki/BFI_Gallery "BFI Gallery"), the contemporary art space dedicated to artists' moving image active at [BFI Southbank](/wiki/BFI_Southbank "BFI Southbank") between 2007 and 2011.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BFI_National_Archive&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

* [National Film Registry](/wiki/National_Film_Registry "National Film Registry") – the registry of films selected by the United States [National Film Preservation Board](/wiki/National_Film_Preservation_Board "National Film Preservation Board") for preservation in the [Library of Congress](/wiki/Library_of_Congress "Library of Congress")
* [List of archives in the United Kingdom](/wiki/List_of_archives_in_the_United_Kingdom "List of archives in the United Kingdom")
* [Lists of film archives](/wiki/Lists_of_film_archives "Lists of film archives")
